raw_input:
[5.0/5] excellent at evening out rosy skin, great smelling tinted mineral sunscreen, great as primer Interesting product! I have used another rose tinted sunscreen before and was intrigued enough by it to try another. This one might be even better. Let me tell you about it in terms of my ROSACEA (type 1: redness, and visible veins/telangiectasias). I would not thinking adding MORE PINK would be at all helpful to my already rosy complexion. But it does a phenomenal job evening out my skin tone. I have fair, cool toned skin so I find this is still a tad dark-- so if you have skin that is even a little darker than very pale, then this should blend in real well. Yet even with the clearly pink mask that it gives me, it looks better than pink and red splotches. I just wouldnt go out in public with this alone. SO, this got me thinking about using it as a BASE layer to another sunscreen, mixing it, or the best use.. using it under make up. It will provide a great foundational canvas for a layer of foundation. Wont need to rely on the make up to even things out. If you have splotchiness, you may like trying this out. That's what appeals greatest to me-- that it evens out my skin tone gently.

Nice smell, even coverage.. makes my skin feel really good. Semi dewy supple feel. My skin is DRY, and this feels good on, Maybe it just plays well with my other skin care products? Oddly.. it has a slight gritty feel while putting it on. Yet doesnt have a gritty feel ON the skin. Not sure what I am feeling. Its odd.. but it doesnt impact overall look, feel, or performance. [3.0/5] Light sunscreen with a rose tint I've liked the different Alteya products that I'd tried but this Rose Face Sunscreen doesn't work well for me. The texture is nice, light and doesn't feel greasy. It offers mineral protection against UVA and UVB rays. I'm Asian and find that the rose tint is a little strong for my skin tone. While I can use a little and blend it in, there are other tinted and non-tinted sunscreens that are a better fit for my needs. Similarly, someone with a more compatible skintone might find this to be a perfect match. [4.0/5] Pink, gritty, doesn't smell like roses at all. But comfortable. I'm giving this 4 stars because it's one of the most comfortably suncreens I've tried. However, there are other things to note. First the scent; I'm obsessed with rosewater, and I expected this to have a rosewater scent. However, it doesn't smell of roses (or like sunscreen) at all; I actually dislike the oddly medicinal smell, but fortunately it disappears quickly. Also, it has a slight, small gritty texture (and I have not encountered in suncreen before); this also doesn't linger. The textured is watery and easy to distribute. The tint is very much the color of calamine lotion (really), but it's watery and thin so it doesn't show up that color once distributed. I have fair, neutral skin, and I think the effect is actually flattering. However, I am not at all sure how well it will or won't server darker skins. As I only have my own skin to test it on, I can't assess how well it will suit darker skin, just note that the slight tint is pink. [4.0/5] Honestly Strange And Frustrating, But The End Result Is Great I really enjoy using the Alteya Organics rose water, so I was very excited to try this rose face organic sunscreen SPF 30. This sunscreen is very unique to me in that it isn’t white, it isn’t simply beige tinted, but looks dusty rose/beige tinted.

So there are definitely some things about this product that I kind of dislike/ am on the fence about, but ultimately, The end result is surprisingly awesome.

What I don’t like-
I usually start with what I like, but I noticed all the things I didn’t like very quickly, so I’ll start with that.

-The pump dispenser. I don’t know what is going on with this thing, but I opened the box and tried pumping a couple times- nothing came out. I thought nothing of it since it usually takes multiple or many pumps initially to get any product into the pump..... but it never came. I started shaking the bottle and then pumping..... nothing. I almost gave up, but then violently started pumping it by hammering the top with my hand-tiny little bits started to splash out. After some more hammering, it seems to start coming out normally with a simple press. The product it pretty thick, so that could have been a factor I suppose? Now it is easier for it to come out, but still stops occasionally. When I press, the product usually comes out at the very end of a full pump.
-Not incredibly easy to spread and rub in properly. It isn’t terrible and I wasn’t too surprised since it is fairly thick and it is sunscreen, but it did require some effort to work it into my skin.
-It feels somewhat grainy/gritty when I’m trying to rub it into my skin. I’m not sure why or if it is just the product I received. I can’t feel any granules on my face after application, but it was definitely there initially which I thought was odd.
-I had a sensation of wanting to wash my hands afterwards. I could definitely feel it lingering on my fingers.

After all that, I felt a little disappointed... but you know what? I really like the end result and the way it makes me look.

What I like-

-Really subtle natural scent- almost like the smell of clay with a hint of rose. I like it. I do see in the ingredients that there is also lavender oil.
-The tinted color... it is like a dusty rose beige. I really like it. I have somewhat tannish olive skin and something about this color is making my skin look good. It is almost like I’m wearing light makeup. Somehow my skin looks brighter and glowy after application.
-Doesn’t feel so heavy as I’m wearing. I’ll admit- the product is thick, feels somewhat grainy with application, and is not the easiest to rub in, but it feels light sitting on my face. Yes, even after washing hands, I can feel it is there when I run my fingers across my face, but it feels soft. There is a little bit of friction like it doesn’t feel completely silky smooth, but I’m not bothered by it.
-Doesn’t leave a white cast. I suppose this isn’t surprising since it isn’t white to begin with.... but I love that. Does it leaves cast? Kind of, but in a good way. Like I said, I feel like it leaves a very slight color that evens out my skin, almost like light makeup.

I really wanted to leave a 3 star rating because the pump is annoying and frustrating, the grittiness is strange, and it isn’t the easiest to rub in, but darn I like how my skin looks afterwards!!! It doesn’t make any sense to me. It almost feels like it would leave my skin feeling and looking greasy/oily, but it doesn’t- my skin looks nice and somewhat dewy.

If the company finds a way to make the pump dispenser work properly and possibly make it feel easier to apply then this would without a doubt be a 5 star product! I also wish the bottle was larger than 50ml, especially considering the hassle of the pump.

Anyway, I hope my review was helpful in some way, and if so. I would love to know!! Thanks!! [5.0/5] My go to sunscreen/foundation base. Have been using this product for years. Evens out my skin tone (62 years old, medium complexion, olive/Mediterranean). Glides on evenly & easily. I use a very light weight liquid foundation over it for a smooth finish, ready to head out. [2.0/5] Two Stars It has color and it has dry texture [3.0/5] Three Stars Just okay, this product has a very pink tint. [5.0/5] Non greasy sun screen Was not greasy. Smells like roses. Seems to work pretty well as a sunscreen.Wish it was a tad tinted lighter.Since I have pale skin.Other than that great!👍 [5.0/5] Flawless I sampled this on my hand at my coop and I wasn't sure about it because of the unusual pink tint, but I bought it because I liked the ingredients. This is my second day using it and I absolutely love it! The pink tint is perfect for my skin and it offers a flawless, beautiful finish. It is a little bit dry and sticky when you put it on and it could rub in easier if you added some oil or mixed it with other sunscreen. All in all, I'm very pleased with this product. Most suncreens have an orange/brown tint that doesn't compliment my skin very well. [5.0/5] Love it! I'm very happy with this product, it's similar to tinted sunscreen from Badger that's no longer available. I hope to buy this again, when it'll be available in USA. [5.0/5] Pls bring it back 🙏🙏 such a beautiful gentle organic sunscreen that didn't leave white cast.. Best sunscreen .. [5.0/5] It works great! This is by far the best sunscreen I’ve ever used. It gives great coverage from the sun, and the rose smell is awesome. Love this product [5.0/5] Very good product Loved it!!!! Yes it does have a pink tint but it is oh so perfect for sun exposure! Even when being in the pool, sea, exposed to the sun and sweat it protects your skin and does not dry it!! Really recommend it. [1.0/5] Bad Best before August 2021.
So sticky that can't be pumped out.
Some hard powder. No one would put this on their face

[4.0/5] Honestly Strange And Frustrating, But The End Result Is Great I really enjoy using the Alteya Organics rose water, so I was very excited to try this rose face organic sunscreen SPF 30. This sunscreen is very unique to me in that it isn’t white, it isn’t simply beige tinted, but looks dusty rose/beige tinted.

So there are definitely some things about this product that I kind of dislike/ am on the fence about, but ultimately, The end result is surprisingly awesome.

What I don’t like-
I usually start with what I like, but I noticed all the things I didn’t like very quickly, so I’ll start with that.

-The pump dispenser. I don’t know what is going on with this thing, but I opened the box and tried pumping a couple times- nothing came out. I thought nothing of it since it usually takes multiple or many pumps initially to get any product into the pump..... but it never came. I started shaking the bottle and then pumping..... nothing. I almost gave up, but then violently started pumping it by hammering the top with my hand-tiny little bits started to splash out. After some more hammering, it seems to start coming out normally with a simple press. The product it pretty thick, so that could have been a factor I suppose? Now it is easier for it to come out, but still stops occasionally. When I press, the product usually comes out at the very end of a full pump.
-Not incredibly easy to spread and rub in properly. It isn’t terrible and I wasn’t too surprised since it is fairly thick and it is sunscreen, but it did require some effort to work it into my skin.
-It feels somewhat grainy/gritty when I’m trying to rub it into my skin. I’m not sure why or if it is just the product I received. I can’t feel any granules on my face after application, but it was definitely there initially which I thought was odd.
-I had a sensation of wanting to wash my hands afterwards. I could definitely feel it lingering on my fingers.

After all that, I felt a little disappointed... but you know what? I really like the end result and the way it makes me look.

What I like-

-Really subtle natural scent- almost like the smell of clay with a hint of rose. I like it. I do see in the ingredients that there is also lavender oil.
-The tinted color... it is like a dusty rose beige. I really like it. I have somewhat tannish olive skin and something about this color is making my skin look good. It is almost like I’m wearing light makeup. Somehow my skin looks brighter and glowy after application.
-Doesn’t feel so heavy as I’m wearing. I’ll admit- the product is thick, feels somewhat grainy with application, and is not the easiest to rub in, but it feels light sitting on my face. Yes, even after washing hands, I can feel it is there when I run my fingers across my face, but it feels soft. There is a little bit of friction like it doesn’t feel completely silky smooth, but I’m not bothered by it.
-Doesn’t leave a white cast. I suppose this isn’t surprising since it isn’t white to begin with.... but I love that. Does it leaves cast? Kind of, but in a good way. Like I said, I feel like it leaves a very slight color that evens out my skin, almost like light makeup.

I really wanted to leave a 3 star rating because the pump is annoying and frustrating, the grittiness is strange, and it isn’t the easiest to rub in, but darn I like how my skin looks afterwards!!! It doesn’t make any sense to me. It almost feels like it would leave my skin feeling and looking greasy/oily, but it doesn’t- my skin looks nice and somewhat dewy.

If the company finds a way to make the pump dispenser work properly and possibly make it feel easier to apply then this would without a doubt be a 5 star product! I also wish the bottle was larger than 50ml, especially considering the hassle of the pump.

Anyway, I hope my review was helpful in some way, and if so. I would love to know!! Thanks!! [5.0/5] My go to sunscreen/foundation base. Have been using this product for years. Seems to work pretty well as a sunscreen.Wish it was a tad tinted lighter.Since I have pale skin.Other than that great!👍 [5.0/5] Flawless I sampled this on my hand at my coop and I wasn't sure about it because of the unusual pink tint, but I bought it because I liked the ingredients. This is my second day using it and I absolutely love it! The pink tint is perfect for my skin and it offers a flawless, beautiful finish. It is a little bit dry and sticky when you put it on and it could rub in easier if you added some oil or mixed it with other sunscreen. All in all, I'm very pleased with this product. response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, this rose-tinted mineral SPF 30 sunscreen from Alteya Organics receives mostly positive feedback, with many customers loving it as a daily sunscreen and makeup base. The most common praise is that it evens out skin tone, feels lightweight and non-greasy, and leaves a subtle, flattering glow. Several reviewers appreciate that it avoids the white cast common with mineral sunscreens. Long-term users find it works well as a foundation primer, and some note it holds up well in water and sweat.

The pink tint is the most frequently discussed feature, and opinions are mixed. Customers with fair to medium skin tones tend to find it flattering, while those with darker or Asian skin tones may find the pink too strong. A few reviewers note it resembles calamine lotion in the bottle but blends out more naturally on the skin.

Common complaints include a slightly gritty texture during application that mostly disappears once rubbed in, a pump dispenser that can be difficult or inconsistent, and a scent that some describe as medicinal rather than rose-like, though it fades quickly. One reviewer received an expired product that was too sticky to use.

The product has a loyal following, with multiple customers expressing disappointment over its limited availability and hoping it returns to the US market.
